rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Petition Of 2614 Sacramento Street, Llc, To Cancel Release The Mechanics Lien
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 13, 2019 line 1. PETITIONER 2614 SACRAMENTO STREET, LLC Notice Of Hearing On Petition Of 2614 Sacramento Street, Llc, To Cancel Release The Mechanics Lien HEARING REQUIRED. Petitioner must provide the Court with a Proof of Service showing that the Petition was served with 15 days notice of the May 30, 2019 hearing. Proof of Service only shows the Notice of Hearing was served. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: SOO MING LAI VS. MARIA NGUYEN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Special Motion To Strike (Anti-Slapp) Cross-Complainants Fifth Cause Of Action For Intentional Infliction Of Emotional Distress
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 13, 2019 line 3. CROSS DEFENDANT ALLEN LAI, GINA CHU Notice Of Motion And Special Motion To Strike (Anti-Slapp) Cross-Complainants Fifth Cause Of Action For Intentional Infliction Of Emotional Distress DENIED in part and GRANTED in part. The motion is DENIED as to paragraphs 24, 51, 52, 53 and items #1 and #3 in the prayer for relief. Paragraph 24 does not contain protected speech. There are numerous unchallenged allegations sufficient to sustain the 5th COA and the resulting damages requests in the paragraphs 51, 52, 53 and items #1 and #3 in the prayer for relief. The motion is GRANTED as to the allegations in paragraphs 20, 21, 22 as incorporated into the 5th Cause of Action against Cross Defendants ALLEN LAI and GINA CHU. The 30 day notice discussed in the paragraphs 20-22 was in furtherance of protected speech. (See CCP §425.16(e).) Cross-complainant failed to meet its burden in showing that paragraphs 20-22 support a claim for recovery of the 5th COA. (See Baral v. Schnitt (2016) 1 Cal.5th 376, 488.) More specifically, the Cross-complainant failed to submit evidence as to why an unlawful detainer filed by SOO MING LAI would support the recovery of an IIED claim against LAI and CHU. Defendant's argument that Plaintiff needed to provide evidence to establish a prima facie case of claims based on both protected and unprotected speech in order for the 5th COA to survive misunderstands the holding of Baral. Under Baral, a party need only provide evidence for claims that arise from protected speech. (Ibid. at 396.) Accordingly, only paragraphs 20-22 are stricken. Because the motion has been granted in part and denied in part, and the results of the motion "were so insignificant that the party did not achieve any practical benefit from bringing the motion" the Court determines in its discretion that cross-defendants were not the prevailing party for the purposes of attorneys fees. (See Mann v. Quality Old Time Service (2006) 139 Cal.App.4th 328, 345 [disapproved by Baral v. Schnitt (2016) 1 Cal.5th 376 on other grounds only].) = (501/CFH)
